title: classroom50/result/v1
description: Machine-readable schema for the result.json an autograder (or runner.py's synthesized error/vacuous/declarative paths) writes per submission. Mirrors the authoritative validators runner.py::validate_result and collect_scores.py::validate_result (cli/gh-teacher/skeleton/dotgithub/scripts/). Rules JSON Schema cannot express, enforced in code: (1) classroom/assignment/owner must match the SOURCE REPO's identity (a hostile result can't land in another student's gradebook); `owner` is the identity anchor (the <username> from the repo name). (2) assignment_type must match the assignment's configured mode. (3) score <= max-score and per-test score <= per-test max-score are cross-field. There is no `usernames` field: who pushed is `submitted_by`, who owns the repo is `owner`, and the credited member list (group only) is resolved by collection. This schema captures only the structural contract.

classroom.description: Classroom short-name. Must equal the source repo's classroom (enforced in code against the repo identity).

assignment.description: Assignment slug. Must equal the source repo's assignment (enforced in code).

assignment_type.description: The assignment's mode, stamped by the runner from the MODE env. Collection cross-checks it against assignments.json `mode` and warns-and-skips on mismatch.

owner.description: The repo owner login (the <username> from <classroom>-<assignment>-<username>). The identity anchor the validators check; for an individual assignment it is the sole credited student, for a group it is the founder whose repo was graded.

submission.description: The submit/<UTC-timestamp>-<short-sha> tag this result graded.

commit.description: Link to the graded commit on the Git host.

release.description: Link to the submission release on the Git host.

review.description: Full-diff (baseline...graded commit) review link; equals `commit` when there is nothing to compare.

datetime.description: UTC instant the result was produced, formatted YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ. Advisory: collect-scores compares it against the assignment `due` to mark a row `late` in scores.json.

score.description: Points earned. Must be <= max-score (cross-field, enforced in code).

max-score.description: Total points available (0 for a vacuous/error result).

tests.description: Per-test outcomes. Empty for a vacuous pass (no autograder configured) or an error result.

submittedBy.description: Optional: the GitHub actor who pushed THIS submission, stamped by the runner from GITHUB_ACTOR/GITHUB_ACTOR_ID. For a group submission the score is credited to every member (collection resolves the member list), but `submitted_by` records who actually pushed this particular submission. Absent on results produced before this field existed.

id.description: The pusher's numeric GitHub user id (GITHUB_ACTOR_ID), or null when unavailable.

testResult.description: One graded test. additionalProperties is true: an autograder.py may attach extra diagnostic fields (e.g. output, message) that the runner and collector preserve verbatim.

test-name.description: Human-readable test name (the declarative grader uses the assignment test `name`).

score.description: Points earned on this test. Must be <= this test's max-score (cross-field, enforced in code).
